title: TraceConfig
description: Single source of truth for trace-injection configuration. Read by mlir-trace-inject, cpp_trace_patch, parse-trace, trace_compare, and the bridge script. Written by trace-prepare. Spec: docs/superpowers/findings/2026-05-05-trace-config-schema.md.

placement.description: Where the kernel's logical (col=0, row=0) corner lives in the physical tile array. Declarative (MLIR-intended) origin; runtime may shift by re-emitting events at absolute coordinates. Trace-compare uses the per-side observed origin in events.json to normalize HW vs EMU when one side runs at start_col!=0.

origin_col.description: Smallest col index across tiles_traced. EMU honors MLIR coordinates literally, so its observed origin matches this value. HW's resource manager may pick a different physical start_col; in that case the per-side events.json carries the runtime-observed origin.

origin_row.description: Smallest row index across tiles_traced. Almost always 0 (shim row) when shim tiles are instrumented, otherwise 2 (first compute row).

schema_version.description: Bump on any breaking change. Tools refuse to load mismatched majors.

test_name.description: Stable identifier (matches mlir-aie test directory name).

src_mlir.description: Absolute path to the original (pre-injection) aie.mlir. The injector reads this; everyone else uses it for cache invalidation.

size_bytes.description: Trace BO size. Multiple of 4 KiB. Both HW and EMU allocate exactly this much.

kernel_arg_slot.description: Authoritative kernel-arg slot for the trace BO. Used by aie.trace.host_config(arg_idx=...), aiex.npu.address_patch arg_idx=..., and kernel.group_id(...) in host code. ALL coordinate systems derive from this.

embedded_in_memref_idx.description: If non-null, the trace BO is NOT a separate kernel arg — it's appended to the existing memref at runtime_sequence index N (mlir-aie's arg_idx=-1 semantics). When set, kernel_arg_slot refers to that memref's slot.

slot.description: Kernel arg position as XRT sees it (matches kernel.group_id(slot)).

name.description: Human-readable name. For BOs, matches the host code's variable (e.g. bo_inA).

role.description: What the BO is for. phantom = aiecc emitted a slot the host doesn't bind; we record it so the mismatch is visible.

ctype.description: C type for scalars (uint64_t, uint32_t, etc.).

memref_idx.description: For data BOs: the index in the runtime_sequence's memref args list (0..N-1). null otherwise.

mode.description: Trace unit mode. Determines event vs PC-anchored vs instruction-execution semantics.

core_grounding.description: Event names always traced on core tiles (e.g. PERF_CNT_0).

core_sweep.description: Per-batch sweep events (variable across runs in a sweep).

memmod_grounding.description: Grounding events for the compute tile's memory-module trace unit (sibling of core trace, packet type Mem).

kind.description: Derived from row: 0 = shim, 1 = memtile, >=2 = core.

events.description: Up to 8 event slots in trace_unit. Decoders read these to label each 4B word.

packet_id.description: Stream-switch packet ID for this tile's trace channel. Matches aie.packet_flow(packet_id).

module.description: Which module's trace_unit (only meaningful for core tiles, which have both).

shim_col.description: Column of the shim tile that drains all trace packets.

shim_dma_channel.description: Shim DMA S2MM channel (0 or 1). Channel 1 is the conventional trace channel.

shim_bd_id.description: Shim BD assigned to drive trace traffic. Defaults to 15.

diagnostics.description: Optional cross-checks. Mismatches are warnings, not errors — they surface drift between schema-derived expectations and actual generated artifacts.
